sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 6-month follow-up visit for a patient who had a heart attack 6 months ago and underwent stenting of the left anterior descending artery.
The patient reports that the medications are helping and they are doing okay, but they are concerned about the number of medications they are taking.
They mention that they have been experiencing dizziness when standing up quickly, which they attribute to either the heart attack or the medication.
The patient also mentions that they have not been following up with the dietitian for their diabetes and have a high A1c level of 60%.
They have a history of kidney disease, stage 3 CKD, and are concerned about the potential effects of medications on their kidneys.
The patient is also experiencing some edema in their legs.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of heart attack, coronary artery disease, and stage 3 CKD.
They have been prescribed multiple medications, including aspirin, brilinta, metoprolol, crestor, and fish oil.
Review of systems: The patient reports dizziness when standing up quickly, but no other symptoms.
Current medications:
Aspirin
Brilinta
Metoprolol 50mg once a day
Crestor 40mg once a day
Fish oil
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 127/80, which is within a normal range.
Physical exam: The patient has a slight 3/6 systolic ejection murmur at the left base and some 1+ nonpitting edema in their legs.
Test Results: The patient's kidney function is stable, and they have a creatinine level that is within normal limits.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has coronary artery disease, stage 3 CKD, and diabetes.
Problem list:
Coronary artery disease; Stage 3 CKD; Diabetes; Mild depression.
Plan:
I will refer the patient to cardiac rehab to help them become more active and confident in exercising again.
I will also refill their metoprolol, crestor, and aspirin.
I will reach out to Dr. Miller to schedule an appointment for the patient to follow up with their nephrologist.
I will order another hemoglobin A1c and refer the patient to a dietitian for dietary education.
I will also refer the patient to psychiatry for difficulty adjusting after their heart attack and possible mild depression.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with the dietitian, nephrologist, and psychiatrist as scheduled.
